Crepis alpina, also known as Alpine Hawk's Beard, is a perennial plant belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate digestive issues, reduce oxidative stress, and promote overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Crepis alpina include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Crepis alpina is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including gastrointestinal issues and respiratory problems.
